0a53d50d37eeea0337b3b1341fbe250dc00731b0.title: Commercial Lease Agreement – Brightwell Dental, Suite 210
0a53d50d37eeea0337b3b1341fbe250dc00731b0.summary: A commercial lease agreement between Stonegate Properties, LP (Landlord) and Brightwell Dental Associates, PLLC (Tenant) for approximately 3,400 square feet of office space at Suite 210, 1800 Brickell Avenue, Miami, Florida. The five-year lease runs from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031, with a starting monthly base rent of $11,900 increasing 3% annually, plus a $23,800 security deposit and an 8.7% proportionate share of operating expenses. The premises are designated solely for dental practice use, with maintenance responsibilities divided between Tenant (interior) and Landlord (structural and common areas).

c771a28c60e8834566868760222cc22524231fa6.title: Executive Employment Agreement – Dr. Marcus A. Thornton
c771a28c60e8834566868760222cc22524231fa6.summary: An executive employment agreement between Pinnacle Healthcare Systems, Inc. and Dr. Marcus A. Thornton appointing him as Chief Medical Officer with a base salary of $425,000 and a 40% target annual bonus, commencing March 1, 2026. The agreement outlines a three-year initial term with automatic renewal, severance provisions, and termination conditions. It also includes non-competition and non-solicitation restrictions for 12 months post-employment within a 100-mile radius of Company facilities, along with confidentiality and intellectual property obligations.

2619a6a98289a8cf03ce76938481de69f051b3cb.title: Non-Disclosure Agreement – Hartwell & Meridian
2619a6a98289a8cf03ce76938481de69f051b3cb.summary: A non-disclosure agreement between Hartwell Capital Group, LLC (Disclosing Party) and Meridian Advisory Partners, Inc. (Receiving Party) governing the protection and use of confidential business information shared for the purpose of evaluating a potential business relationship. The Receiving Party is obligated to maintain strict confidentiality, refrain from unauthorized disclosure or use, and return or destroy all confidential materials upon termination. The agreement has a three-year term, is governed by New York law, and requires binding arbitration for dispute resolution.

3613712b76349f5090b8b6ebe5b70a277992dd69.title: Software License Agreement – DataSync Enterprise Suite
3613712b76349f5090b8b6ebe5b70a277992dd69.summary: A software license agreement between Nexon Technologies, Inc. (Licensor) and Caldwell Manufacturing Solutions (Licensee) governing the use of DataSync Enterprise Suite version 4.2. The agreement grants a non-exclusive, non-transferable license for up to 50 workstations in exchange for an annual fee of $48,000, while imposing restrictions on modification, reverse engineering, and redistribution. It also disclaims all warranties and limits Licensor's cumulative liability to fees paid in the preceding twelve months.

08cd0c1746f1b76bb906d2349e7c3583fd451871.title: Core Trial Bundle Index - February 2025
08cd0c1746f1b76bb906d2349e7c3583fd451871.summary: This is the Core Trial Bundle Index for a civil court case with a trial window of 12-20 February 2025, totalling 453 pages. It catalogues nine documents across two sections: Case Management Documents (including agreed case summary, chronology, list of issues, trial timetable, and reading list) and Statements of Case (including a claim form, amended particulars of claim, amended defence, and a defendant's request for further information under CPR Part 18). The statements of case span documents dated between January 2022 and September 2023.

514a513b36d78ae6a93f836283266469dc40a82b.title: Court Bundle Index - Civil Litigation Case
514a513b36d78ae6a93f836283266469dc40a82b.summary: This is page 2 of a 453-page court bundle index listing documents in a civil litigation matter. It includes pleadings, witness statements, court orders, schedules of loss, and skeleton arguments spanning from 2016 to 2025. The bundle covers both the original proceedings between Adaptainer and the Claimant, as well as a live application by the Defendant to strike out the Witness Statement of Paul Tilley.

4cce4c01a69e05af2ca4ec6b1920ef83ccf30ebe.title: Litigant AI Weekly Report – 21 to 27 Sep 2025
4cce4c01a69e05af2ca4ec6b1920ef83ccf30ebe.summary: A consulting weekly report covering 16 billable hours of work on the Litigant AI project, including completion of a UK CaseLaw crawler downloading 67,000 cases (134,000 files, ~28-29 GB), statistical sampling validation of the dataset, and initial development of a UK Legislation crawler targeting 33,000 legislative documents. The report also outlines ongoing NLP preprocessing and ingestion work for CaseLaw cases into a database for integration with third-party LLM engines, with upcoming tasks including full deployment of the legislation crawler and cloud-based big data processing.

072d335fcd176b506c84652a7bd942ac65b3bd82.title: Litigant AI Weekly Report - OCR Development
072d335fcd176b506c84652a7bd942ac65b3bd82.summary: A weekly progress report from Windrose Consulting covering work completed between August 4-6, 2025 on the Litigant AI project. The report details dataset review and selection of 627 scanned receipts and invoices for OCR testing, along with development of an OCR proof-of-concept using AWS Rekognition achieving 80-90% accuracy. Upcoming tasks include testing on actual scanned legal cases, digital PDF data extraction, and legal data cleaning and preparation for AI ingestion.

697c61086e59f69e6f15db824c55dc71433b8611.title: Litigant AI Weekly Report – Oct 26–31 2025
697c61086e59f69e6f15db824c55dc71433b8611.summary: A weekly consulting report from Windrose Consulting covering 16 billable R&D hours for the Litigant Legal Assistant AI project. It details progress on the LangChain and LangGraph multi-agent framework setup, PostgreSQL LitigantAIDB integration, and the Legal AI Assistant conversation flow development. The report includes a process flowchart, sprint board status, and notes that a first demo is expected the following week.

d0c9e0a66ce30d1b8b01919b6c9cfabb41f5fad1.title: Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release
d0c9e0a66ce30d1b8b01919b6c9cfabb41f5fad1.summary: A settlement agreement between Greenfield Construction Corp. (Claimant) and Vantage Commercial Builders, LLC (Respondent) resolving a construction subcontract dispute related to a commercial office complex in Columbus, Ohio. Respondent agrees to pay $275,000 in structured installments, and both parties mutually release all claims arising from the subcontract and project. The agreement also provides for dismissal of an existing AAA arbitration proceeding, along with non-disparagement and confidentiality obligations.

9b01e9207510c8e1121a58f034df16a9e3fd1ba8.title: Schedule Of Loss   Bundle Of Evidence
9b01e9207510c8e1121a58f034df16a9e3fd1ba8.filename: Schedule of Loss - Bundle of Evidence.pdf
